citations.description: Ticket comment ts 1783906290844586001 (Joe Oakhart): provides structural criterion-count ratios (2-call probe vs ~8–12-call full review = ~75–83% fewer calls) as the 'measurement,' explicitly deferring empirical per-run token/latency to a post-deploy deferred ticket (72b6).
ADR measurement section contains real run data for full reviews (9 LLM calls, ~172s–~337s) but these pre-date this ticket; probe run is estimated analytically at ~3 LLM calls, not measured.

Ticket 'What' section: 'Close the original opt-in condition: measure the token/latency saving of a drift-refresh probe vs a full re-review on a representative ticket (an f1fa-shaped fixture: signed PASS, plan unchanged, HEAD advanced) and record the numbers on this ticket.'

findings.criterion: The token/latency measurement (probe vs full review on a representative ticket) is recorded in a comment on this ticket.
findings.detail: The comment recorded at timestamp 1783906290844586001 provides a structural/analytical argument about criterion-count ratios (2 agent-tier calls for the probe vs ~8–12 for a full review), but does NOT contain actual measured token counts or wall-clock latency from running a probe vs a full re-review on a specific representative f1fa-shaped fixture (signed PASS, plan unchanged, HEAD advanced). The 'What' section explicitly requires 'measure the token/latency saving of a drift-refresh probe vs a full re-review on a representative ticket...and record the numbers on this ticket.' The ADR (lines 76–84) contains real run data for full reviews but not for probe runs, and those pre-existed this ticket. No actual probe-run numbers (tokens, wall-clock) appear in any ticket comment.
findings.remediation: Run the drift-refresh probe against a real or fixture-based f1fa-shaped ticket (signed PASS, plan unchanged, HEAD advanced) and record the actual token counts and wall-clock time for both the probe path and a full re-review on that same ticket in a new ticket comment, naming the fixture ticket id and observed numbers.

citations.description: Lines 90–93: 'Because the saving is real but bounded (the 2 probe criteria are themselves agent-tier), the path ships **opt-in** until a project confirms the per-token saving on its own representative tickets, at which point the default can be flipped on in a follow-up.' — undated default-OFF framing not covered by the dated-note exception.
Line 64 was correctly updated: 'verify.progressive_drift_refresh (**default ON** since 2026-07-12, epic a37b; explicit false backs out)' — but lines 91–93 contradict this.
Lines 71–74 are inside the dated '(Update 2026-07-11:...)' blockquote and are correctly excepted by the AC's 'dated ADR notes excepted' clause.

findings.criterion: The stale-framing sweep leaves zero default-OFF claims about drift-refresh in src/, tests/, docs/ (dated ADR notes excepted).
findings.detail: docs/adr/0002-code-drift-invalidation.md line 91 still contains the sentence 'the path ships **opt-in** until a project confirms the per-token saving on its own representative tickets, at which point the default can be flipped on in a follow-up.' This is an undated default-OFF framing claim about drift-refresh in docs/ that the sweep should have updated. It is NOT inside the dated update note ('Update 2026-07-11:...' at lines 71–74) that the AC explicitly excepts — it is original prose in the Measurement subsection (lines 86–93) that was left unchanged. The same section's preamble (line 64) was correctly updated to 'default ON since 2026-07-12,' creating an internal contradiction within ADR 0002.
findings.remediation: Update docs/adr/0002-code-drift-invalidation.md lines 90–93 to reflect the default-ON status, e.g. replace 'the path ships **opt-in** until a project confirms the per-token saving on its own representative tickets, at which point the default can be flipped on in a follow-up' with a note that the path shipped default-OFF pending this measurement, and was flipped default-ON on 2026-07-12 (epic a37b) on the basis of the structural criterion-count saving documented here.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
